channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

FUCK LOST ON FPBP

61970841

61988727

kek that was hilarious

61993783
>>61992920

61994101

Why are they proud of this?

61994141
>>61994101
they got approved for the car of they dreams

61994170
>>61984176
>both
>names three things
literally unwatchable

61994267
Shout out to my homie who pooped on a picture of Sasha Grey on his laptop for 20k doge back in 2014!

61994368
>>61976277
tmitbs

61994777
>>61970825

61994788
>>61987657
damn no matter how lucky you might get with investments you'll never party your fucking face off all through the prime of your life like a 19 year old with $15 million

61994944

>he gets rekt over it
>reddit hides his replies with downvotes
Lololol

61995758
>>61994238

did lol

>>61994777
kekked and checked

>>61994379
just sad.

>>61994906

item.title: OIL IS DUMPING! — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OIL IS DUMPING!

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-17 15:34 UTC

OP

Iran has allowed the first oil tanker to pass through the Strait of Hormuz after the shipment was paid for in Chinese yuan.

The ‘Karachi’ oil tanker, an Aframax-class vessel carrying Das crude from Abu Dhabi, crossed the Strait of Hormuz safely with its AIS (Automatic Identification System) activated.

It is the first non-Iranian oil tanker to do so today without any incident

Early replies (first 3)

61992030
>paid for in yuan

Westbros...

61992168
>>61992010
>Oil will dump because China gets access to oil which it already had through India (actually Russia)

61992268
>>61992010
ONE SHIP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992298
Imagine if everyone just starts paying in yuan and this is how USD dies. Wouldn't that be funny haha
...not for my fucking portfolio it won't be

61992382
>>61992010
I voted for this

61992933
>>61992268
It starts with ONE SHIP

61992940
surely our allies won't pay in chink dollars..r-right burger bros?

61992941
>>61992298

61994756
>>61992298
It would be quite funny.

61994778
>>61992010
Why would they want a weaker currency...?

61994783
>>61992010
Iran could do something smart like charging ships $100,000 equiv in yuan to pass thru the straight and build a couple of those garage drones per ship. That would even gain them more sympathy from the NeverTrumps.

61994792
>>61992298
What if the US started bombing the ships that Iran let through in retaliation? That will be hilarious also.

61995468
>>61994792
As if the US wasn't already hated enough by the rest of the world.

61995477
>>61995468
I speak for the rest of the world and we love Israel more

61995535
>>61992298
The US is pure evil and deserves to collapse, let's be real

61995556
>>61995477
Wow now that you mention it, I never realized that I loved Israel that much. I'll call my newborn Jeffrey Benyamin Shlomo in honor to that great, or should I say chosen, country.

61995627
>>61992010
Why did Chingchongs ban Twitter yet have so many propaganda accounts on there?

61995672
>>61995535
And other countries are not? Most countries aka politicians are the same, unfortunately. It's just that the US has more power currently.

61995683
>>61992010
>>61992030
>>61992298
>>61992940
>>61994783
Where is the evidence it was "paid for in Chinese Yuan"

Logistically how do you even verify that as an Iranian? Couldn't Jews just say "ok fine we paid in yuan" but not actually, kinda like how you have to lie on a resume to get a job?

How reliable is "China pulse"?

61995724
>>61995683
>How reliable is "China pulse"?
As reliable as your favorite news outlet.

item.title: REMEMBER THIS THREAD — 36 replies — 78.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
REMEMBER THIS THREAD

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-17 15:18 UTC

OP

WASTE 95% OF YOUR WEALTH ON AN6THING NOW. IF YOU ARE RICH, THERE IS A REVOLUTION AGAINST THE RICH COMING...

Early replies (first 3)

61987006
>>61987000
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

61987019
>>61987000
Che...chee.. checked.

61987033
>>61987000
I wish. People are too brainwashed pr comfortable to give a shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61988091
>>61987000
Lmao the copeunists are coping.
>reee I hate the rich because...
>uh why do I hate them again?
>oh yeah because I wanna be rich!!
>Reeeeeeeee
Shut up retard

61988103
>>61988091
how much do you make a year?

61988108
>>61987082
You are on the wrong side, goober.
Reconsider your views.

61988111
>>61988103
I make 696,969 a year selling dragon dildos to weak faggot communist fembois online

61988116
>>61988091
Start prepping and head for the woods

61988124
>>61988103
He isn’t poor. Thats for sure.

61988126
>>61988111
why don't poorfags ever answer this question?

61988132
>>61988124
it's hilarious that he thinks $700k/yr is rich. like that's his tongue-in-cheek response. >>61988111 i'm guessing he makes between $60k and $80k a year IRL.

61988144
>>61988126
For all you know my answer could be 99% true. For all you know, I really do make over 600k a year selling things to weak faggot commie fembois who are chronically online.
>>61988132

61988158
>>61988144
your fixation on 4chud memes and the "i've been found out, better attach an image to my post" behavior indicate otherwise.

someone who likely makes considerably more money than you is commanding you to answer a question. you'd better listen to your superiors.

61988165
>>61988158
Aww, that's cute. Your larp is funny. Someone's a little insecure about their wealth, aren't they?

61988211
>>61988132
Its definitely rich.

61988237
>>61988073
>>61987000
>>61987033

It's really all fantasy. Excluding a handful of high profile billionaires and celebrities you'd hardly know who to target. The rich are well protected in gated communities with private security and rapid police response if you did try to target big homes. Of course if you did target consumption the rich are quite capable of living with the appearance of restraint. I'm worth over $30MM and you couldn't pick me out as such most days

61988246
>>61988237
>the rich are untouchable gods!!! they are beyond any and all physical harm!!!!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V2iGJF0v0GI
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6sCioKnpHdY

61988409
>>61988237
> and you couldn't pick me out as such most days
Cause you don’t look good.
Growing up in a nice area, as kids, we would joke about richer kids and how richer kids looked different.

61988437
>>61988237
The only time rich people don’t look rich is when they are old.
Young rich people always look rich.

61988995
>>61988237
Anon, even the way you talk exposes you.
Also, you think you are safe, but you have no clue. These days tech can get someone really far... Just look at how wars are fought now, and how easy is to dox anyone in the world.

61992388
>>61987033
That's where Luigi's come in. Realistically speaking if the billionaires started getting whacked it would be steps closer to a revolution.
The cattle class are docile as a whole, so the other direction would be fear and chaos followed by an authoritarian Jewish police state

61992415
>>61988091
You can only be super rich if Jews let you, it's their platform, industry, and ultimately their country from the top down.
The only thing the cattle class can achieve is finding the nich shady spot on the plantation and milk it.

Amish maxing is the final solution to the societal disease

61992434
>He doesn't know all revolutions are started by the rich

61992443
>>61987000
Haahah if Im rich ill move somewhere im poor
Checkmate

61992488
>>61987000
Nice trips checked.
But nothing ever happens Anon. Least of all to the rich.

61993880
>>61992388
Clearly a lot of people cheered for that guy, but he basically sacrificed his life for nothing.
It has to be a collective action by anonymous people. That would give legitimacy and would also make rich people, politicians and their puppets shit their fucking pants

61993886
>>61987000
The kind of revolutionaries I'd be afraid of are on the same side as me. It's fine.

61995110
>>61988246
look at how the rothschild kike didn't even care about being confronted. He probably had a 5 snipers, 10 secret service fags disguised as civils, and 5 demons protecting him at all times.

61995115
>>61992434
Also, this. Not a single revolution has been started by a poorfag. Its always a figure sponsored by rich factions, or a kike.

61995465
>>61987006
This number includes IRAs and 401ks and doesn't take into account that if the owners tried to get to their assets, they'd owe the govt almost half of them. I wonder what the number is if you take taxes into account

61995581
We voted in a billionaire as our president.

Grow up man.

61995597
>>61987000
Poorfags can't overthrow the rich. Revolutions are done by millionaires against billionaires as poors die on both sides of the barricade

61995664
>>61988409
And what about the people you didn’t know were rich. You didn’t counter his point.

item.title: US economy is finished — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
US economy is finished

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-17 15:41 UTC

OP

US economy is finished

Early replies (first 3)

61994908
umm sweaty stock buybacks are bullish

61994911
bullish frfr

61994915
$15 billion is fucking nothing

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994928
>>61994905
so? Treasure money movements are not market oriented but government expenditure dependent. This just shows tariffs have yielded so much cash for the US to pay off its debt obligations ahead of schedule.
I have little doubt im interpreting this incorrectly.

61995140
>>61994905
>getting out of foreign debt is bad
lol shalom rabbi

61995161
>issue debt at one maturity
>Buyback at a different maturity
Seemsgood

61995167
None of this shit is real anyway

61995376
>>61994905
>$15 billion buyback
>they have been borrowing $50 billion every week for the last three months

I am not sure this is as great as it sounds

61995396
>sold all my municipal bonds
>now black people will never have running water

61995656
>>61995167
This. Its fake money being lent out and fake money used to pay it back.
I cant believe anyone still believes in the monetary system anymore.
If you believe the paper money we use is anything but a meme you are dealing in the real world.

61995666
>>61995656
Let’s just switch back to gold stanturd, surely the 1% of people holding 99% of physical gold won’t cause any issues.

61995754
>>61994915
/thread

They issue between $70 - $100 billion just in 1 month treasury bills every single week.

item.title: What do I do? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do I do?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-17 15:44 UTC

OP

>35 soon
>120k NW
Do I just keep paupermaxxing and dumping all my savings into my brokerage account + maxxing my Roth every year?

I save about 2k a month. I’ve become a bit of a stock picking fag, but I want to know if I should just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market. My IQ is about 107 and I’m a lazy and unambitious retard so my only “skill” is pauperism

Early replies (first 3)

61995651
>>61995637
>Roth
>thinking you’ll have the privilege to live to retirement age
Bigger scam than life insurance

61995663
>>61995637
Wait until you have 200k and retire in Latin America/SEA/non-EU Eastern Europe.
>nooo I don't want to live in first world
You said "unambitious" right there. Pick the easy life or continue to struggle the rest of your life, up to you

61995677
>>61995663
That's my plan as well, I thought I would need 300 or 400k though, do you reckon 200k is enough?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995685
>>61995637
120k is literally nothing

61995716
>>61995637
Market is overinflated. Just buy 5,000 Link for 40k. And your rich by 2028 at most.

61995720
>>61995677
1. Subtract your current age from average life expectancy age
2. Workout an inflation adjusted annual budget based on the lifestyle you want in the country you want to live in
3. Multiply the cost from 2 by the years in 1
4. You now have how much money you’ll need to live there indefinitely, or at least some sort of approximation

61995734
>>61995677
200k is enough for soft retirement, let it stack up whilst you work part time remote or some shit

61995748
>>61995637
Test.

61995750
>>61995637
>just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market
Retirement accounts should be in index funds or retirement date funds, especially for midwits.
If you're all ready maxing out your 401k and IRA and have anything left over, you can gamble with that.

61995764
>>61995750
I’m only maxxing out my Roth. ~2k goes in my brokerage

item.title: IT EITHER GOES UP HERE OR ITS FUCKING OVER — 4 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT EITHER GOES UP HERE OR ITS FUCKING OVER

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-17 15:25 UTC

OP

IT EITHER GOES UP HERE OR ITS FUCKING OVER

Early replies (first 3)

61995669
>>61995654
Nah

61995675
>>61995654
Prepare the rope

61995689
nooo btc can't pullback and consolidate it has to keep going up up up!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995694
It went up to get me liquidated and now we're crashing hehe. This is a fucking simulation inside my brain. None of you exist.

To the guys in the upper reality, you can just stop. I got it. Your lies don't work anymore.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 68 replies — 40.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-03-17 14:05 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61966637
back to $1?

61968186
>>61966637
>I DOT buy, I DOT care
>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

item.title: cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices… — 353 replies — 39.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 353 • Updated: 2026-03-17 06:42 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/
>>61991543

Early replies (first 3)

61992012
You forgot WHAT?!

61992015
Strip for me

61992017
TRUMP'S MINION BESSENT IS MANIPULATING THE MARKET. THERE IS A GAP BETWEEN FUTURES AND REALITY. AND REALITY ALWAYS WINS.

SCUM!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992654
why are we up? what changed?

61992656
>>61992647
If you don’t want this house of cards to collapse you’re unironically evil.

61992659
They're bombing McDonald's again.

61992660
>>61992438
did he really say that?

61992661
the 4 figure oil baggies are starting to lash out

61992662
>>61992647
I don't fantasize. Bad shit happens all the time.

61992663
>>61992639
I'm still down kek need to be back over 50. But it doesn't matter, this will be worth hundreds soon enough.

61992664
>>61992654
Tuesday dumparacha in play

61992665
>>61992651
>>61992652
Now kiss and make up, jeets
We're all good

61992669
>hurr theres ww3 happening but ill long stocks and short oil

61992670
>>61992148
Ex chad here, 3.5 years for me. I have a body count in the high 20s, so my ego doesn't really have anything to prove. My long term gf dumping me kinda just melted my brain. Idk what happened, but I have brutal social anxiety after that. Getting dumped probably made my brain turn off all the serotonin and dopamine levers as some maladaptive survival mechanism

61992672
>>61992654
I bought

61992673
war is bullish, oil shocks are bullish

61992674
Remember when Oil went negative for a couple of days? That was fun.

https://youtu.be/LUpV2LSXn80?si=xqwrSdmRQglFfgh6

61992678
>>61992663
relax, its one of the few comapnies who is solving the energy question with the data center infra, we're gucci, just hold and average down if you can

61992679
>>61992676
>>61992676
>>61992676

61992680
>>61992669
There is?

61992681
>>61992669
It's literally shills trying to contain the damage.

61992684
>>61992669
>WWIII is happening
>Look outside
>Suns out, birds are chirping, the sky is blue and the grass is green
>Check monitor
>OIL dumping
Lmao.

61992696
markets are getting extremely sharty, low liquidity, flailing market throwing a tantum that it has to go down

61992720
>President Trump is mulling strikes against key Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, according to US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz

61992724
>>61992670
My body count is over 50+ and I gave up on women about 2020.
Been a wanker since.

61992726
>>61992660
Yes

61992871
>>61992678
I literally said it will be 100s lol

61992877
Oil sisters... its over...
We will be oil baggies forever...
$92 and falling...

61993086
Im officially an oil bagholder. Please laugh at me

61993581
>>61992048
Hope you set a stop loss lol

61993590
>>61992049
Just hold you fucking moron. Have some fucking patience, geez!

61993596
>>61992053
A covid-lite 2.0 economy that will give me another chance to make fuck loads of money from a small investment in oil.

None of those other things really matter on a direct personal level if I make fuckloads of money. Oil dropping to the floor is the entry point I've been waiting for.

61994621
>>61992088
Oh wow, you drew a thought bubble showing that she's thinking about fried chicken.

That's really creative anon!

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 72 replies — 27.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-03-17 13:10 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61979017
>>61979005
the thread was about Ripple, and this freak started manically fudding Link out of nowhere >>61972246

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

61994433
>>61993358
No, you literal ironic fucking retard, you do not. Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched for their institutional client through the exchange with it to hit the order book at the exact same time, exactly equating the water analogy I mentioned. Insane you are pretending to not understand this.
>>61993959
Something isn't word salad just because you are unable to respond to it and that frustrates you. You are not entitled to automatic rightness and editor of reality fit to render things incoherent which aren't. Ripple already has TVL despite literally being designed to not have L1 smartcontracts etc. Lol attacing us for having byzantine agreement instead of proof of stake/work is not a deep attack and the funniest thing of your reply is you mirroring the terminology I use. You try to start it by having me be dismissed as incoherent and not considered because you can't address my points (conceding you are insecure in your argument/position) and you then supplicate yourself to me mirroring my language use signaling that you understand my competence and wish to try to conform to it and internalize it by imitative association. You can't fake it til you make it here and you demonstrate yourself to be a literal NPC. Enjoy being a literal cult member to a tech that solves no problem, and inserts friction into the broader software solution as a literal jewish rent seeking fee insertion with no purpose beyond this and being sold to you to fund the software company. Just XRP's recent move over the past few days of +11% up is several billion dollars more than link's entire marketcap. Pic related is since the Trump pump.

61995144
>>61994433
>Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched
Yes, that's how selling and buying works.
You're in here pretending that Ripple invented the order book, holy kek.

This is the level of IQ keeping Ripple afloat.

>>61993484
I remember a time when saying something that stupid got you dogpiled on here. Did this guy simply out-retard everyone through attrition, like he did with you?

61995184
>>61972246
the absolute irony of complaining about the middleman while being completely superflous in a world of atomic swaps

or what, do you think institutions will swap their stablecoins with XRP inbetween? lol good thing we have XRP, as we all know, USDT (usd) to EURC (euro) isn't possible with this godlike 8R1DGE CURR3NCY

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 89 replies — 22.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-03-17 11:45 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61976925
>>61957902
I'm with you but I've done nothing but lose money on WEAT. Consider that this won't effect yields until next planting season, as farmers largely already have their fertilizer for this season. Do you think equilibrium cannot be established before then? If not then there are better plays to make. Markets aren't operating rationally given the oil price is still pricing in the war being over tomorrow.

61977020
>>61976925
I guess anons in this thread, along with further research have convinced me to rotate into fertilizer stocks for now - and keep an eye on WEAT because after the fertilizer stocks it’ll pump too

61977928
>>61976522
I don’t know anything man. I’m a retard same as you. But I am glad we have a thread on biz with some actual dirty alpha in it for a change, instead of the retardium that usually goes on here. Listen to the dudes spouting the fertilizer stocks for now - rotate into commodities when it’s clear the pump is on in weeks to months.

$MOS is a clear winner this and next week

61978236
Amazing post OP. I immediately bought a bunch of fertilizer stocks after this post and am up double digit gains. I also bought WEAT and CORN just for shits and giggles

61978269
you guys are dumb. the fertilizer producers have to battle high gas prices as well. all their profits will be eaten up by higher input costs and they will have to eat some of that cost because they never pass it on completely

61979022
Is there like a single grain ETF? I don't want to pick between what and corn

61979260
>>61977020
I bought mos in pre market it then immediately dumped on open. My MIC stocks and mining stocks crab, gold is down again. If everything trends with Nasdaq nothing really matters.

61979278
>>61979022
Invesco's DBA etf is a broader agricultural etf.

61980793
>>61979260
It moved 20% in a few days, you're a tard for not waiting for the profit taking dump.

61981087
>>61978269
What do you think about Wheat?

61984637
>>61957787
Thanks anon.
In addition to calls, I might just buy some actual 50-lb bags of hard winter wheat.

61984661
>>61972169
But if it's dry again this summer in the Midwest, there won't be much corn either. I milk cows at a dairy farm on the weekends. (A lot of dairy farms use their own cows' manure to grow corn, onions, and hay, to feed the cows.) Last year it was so dry that farmers only produced about half the hay and half the corn they usually do, so they had to buy externally. This winter there was less precipitation as well.

61984669
>>61984661
Not onions - basedbeans
The corn provides calories and the basedbeans protein

61984675
>>61984669
Fuck SPELLCHECKER.
BASEDBEANS!
NOT ONIONS NOT BASEDBEANS whatever the fuck that is

61984681
S O Y B E A N S

61985150
>>61984681
:D

61986226
>straits closed
>no one's talking about the logistics of transportation
Houthis are going to shut down the suez canal. Freight fees are about to go to astronomical highs
Go long on freight companies

61986604
Yeah, the real play here is fertilizer. Most specifically urea based fertilizers. 50% of ALL global urea based fertilizer goes through the straight. Furthermore, it takes a good amount of oil to make it. The vast majority of this is made in asia, whose oil supply is totally fucked now. So, you need an oil company in a country that supplies its own oil, has plenty of open ports, and is not sanctioned to all hell and back. You're looking for an american company with maximum urea manufacturing capacity, which is CF Holdings. See pic related.

61986970
>>61957787
>The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia
No it doesn't. The Haber-Bosch process uses hydrogen and nitrogen. Natural gas can be used to produce the hydrogen, but the hydrogen can also be produced by other means.

Natural gas is not "the" foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer. It is one option out of several. In China, for example, it is more common to produce hydrogen by using coal than by using natural gas. Of course, for plants that rely on natural gas, it is not possible to quickly convert to alternative sources of hydrogen, so there will be a supply shock.

>There are no strategic fertilizer reserves.
Not that it changes your central thesis, but this statement is not really true. For example, China has strategic sulfur reserves. Sulfuric acid is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers. However, those reserves are not large compared to global demand, and China has restricted exports.

61986973
>>61986604
>Most specifically urea based fertilizers.
All nitrogen fertilizers will be affected, not just urea. It is not just nitrogen fertilizers either. A large chunk of sulfur goes through the strait. Sulfur is used in the production of phosphate fertilizers

61986988
>>61958421
>You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer.
Technically speaking, sodium nitrate for use in nitrogen fertilizer can be mined. However it is not a viable production method in this day and age.

61987074
CBOT Wheat has been strongly correlating with crude oil since the start of the month. It's been in an uptrend since the beginning of the year.

For those with futures trading accounts, a $1.00/ bu move in CBOT Wheat equals $5000. Margin is around $1800.

61987171
>>61986604
Any good etfs for urea?

61991205
<bump>

61992085
>fertilizer stocks getting plowed
thanks for nothing dickheads

61992485
>>61992085
It's supposed to be a trade you hold for 6-12 months, moron.

61994353
>>61992085
Farmers in the northern hemisphere already bought what they needed for the season. The time to buy was last October. Look at the chart for $soil etf. Now is a good time to buy crop futures, they will be maturing in 6 months.

61994493
>>61957787
Copy pasting ChatGPT should be punishable by death.

61994812
>>61984637
I eat actual wheat grain as a basic porridge with some meat and seasoning. It smells sweet and nutty and you actually feel full and energetic. Unlike "bread" it actually has the bran and all the other good stuff that corpos strip out of it.

61994973
Guys are we looking at a breakout in Cotton!?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-03-17 14:17 UTC

OP

And just like that LINK (ticker: LINK) will never again be below $10.000

Early replies (first 3)

61993294

>down 80%

61993300

61993313

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993353

61993363
>>61993285

61993365
>>61993285
It's over bros, our Chainlink journey ends here. The Great Reset timeline has come at a crossroad with humanity. The magic is gone, the memes are stale, the shills have been uncovered, the agendas laid bare for all to see, i-i-it's over marines...only tears from here.

61993372
>>61993353
Nah $14.88

61993377
>>61993363
i wonder how that fag nicu is feeling

Hes down a lot already

61993399
>>61993285
pop the champagne bottle, stink has breached $10 in 2026
just another 80+% to go, sprinkle on another 20% to adjust from inflation since then too

61993419
seriously though wtf
from chainlinkdog

"There is no Chainlink network without the LINK token

Users pay in LINK to use Chainlink services (or pay in their preferred method, which is automatically converted to LINK) and network service providers are compensated in LINK, similar to how L1 gas coins function

LINK is also staked to back network security and addresses the fundamental chicken and egg problem of bootstrapping a decentralized network by funding oracle reward subsidies for nodes, analogous how blockchains use block reward subsidies for validators"

... link is seriously never going to a high price is it?

at least it's not cripple though... right?

61993429
>>61993285
Kek, fuddies are SEETHING!

61993430
>>61993399
>80%
Kek, you wish retard!

61993433
>>61993285
I'm thinking we've all just made it. This can't be stopped frens!

61993434
>>61993353
Can’t wait for the 12.65 thread desu

61993454
>>61993434

61993471
>>61993433
Holy shit boys we did it

61993528
>everything pumps
>/biz/ brags about LINK pumping with the market
Every time

61993567
>>61993528
>everything dumps
>/biz/ brags about LINK dumping with the market
link board
suck it up

61993576
link is underperforming shib and doge on the week

61993579
Its crazy seeing people post stuff about "wait until 12". Reminds me that i saw the same posts almost a decade ago, but back then ppl were posting stuff like "were so early bros people dont even know what smartcontracts are" but now people dont even pretend the 'technology' matters and are more forthcoming that its just gambling with speculation

61993630

61993681
It's literally below $10 right now lmao.

61994366
you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown you're brown

61994396

>and crashing

61994405
>>61994366

ew

61994500

61994504

61995302

61995082

imagine living in your mom's basement forever lmao

61995141

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995156
>>61995071
probably yes.

61995172
>>61995071
Everyone is convinced we're going to 40-60k$ and that that's going to be the bottom of the bear market... EVERYONE. Now you may take a wild guess what's about to happen, right?
The market is designed to take as much money from as many people as many times possible.

61995223

61995440
>>61995172

61995500

61995506

61995511

61995561
>>61995071

61995573
>>61995082

61995604
>>61995071

61995607

item.content:encoded:

OP

61981675

61981683

61981696
>>61981675

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990792
>>61990339
It's bots and shills, none of these people actually have a trading account. Certain political interests are paying bots and jeets to shit up every board.

Don't listen to a fucking thing anyone says unless they're providing screenshots of their actions.

61990829
>>61990792
I unironically hope the 2-week special combat mission ends soon so we can get back to our usual shills, jeets and bots. they were a bit calmer

61990840
>>61990829
The two week special combat operation is looking to last years at this point. My best guess is that the leaders of China and India are looking at food riots and total economic meltdown in 6 months. This forces them into military action or making some sort of deal with Iran.

I think the deal is more likely where they will pay a toll to have their oil released in exchange for cash/armaments. And since these nations cannot simply shut off the oil this deal will be made, regardless of what the US says.

61990861
>>61981670
WHY TF OIL DUMPING?!?!?!
this was supposed to be free money and yet I keep losing...

61990884
>>61990861

We haven't even begun to feel actual pain this is just speculation.

61990890
>>61990840
My portfolio can't stand this commotion. 2026/2027 was supposed to be a bullmarket according to Gann's Supercycle astrology ;____;

61990975
>>61990890
The assassination of Khomeni is looking like a generational foreign policy blunder you just have to roll with it.

61991002
>>61990083
>>61990078
nothing ukraine has achieved has been possible without NATO and the USA's direct and massive support in arms, material and leadership, the only thing Ukraine ever supplied was bodies for the meat grinder. Its disgusting frankly.

Ukraine lost the russian war on day 1, they lost all their jets before they left the ground, most of their bases were trashed and the majority of their vehicles were taken out before they even started fighting. It was then that NATO and us army forces pretending to be nato took over.

61991018
>>61990792
i have trading accounts on shares, but i don't really discuss them much, as every tip i ever got from here, and actually followed through on, was a dud and i lost money on it kek. Most of the posters here are clueless retards, i just like to stay up to date on the geopolitical-economic fronts and make my decisions based off of that.

61991050
>>61991018
Well yeah if you are going to make a play on oil getting to $150 you have to realize that it's a super risky move and don't get butthurt if there's suddenly a deal made and you lose all your money.

61991074
>>61983193
Yeah uh private credit definitely isn't imploading

61991122
>>61991018
>stay up to date on geopolitics and make decisions based on that
How's that fairing?

61991150
>>61991002
While I'm not pro-Ukraine or pro-Russia, I can't help but feel your pic related is directionally correct, although the Russian government also pushes globohomo "we need more niggers in our cities because muh diversity" bullshit in its own way.

61991170
>>61991002
Muscovy is already Snowmalia

61991172
>>61983218
Wow! What an active imagination you have. And what'll happen next, Junior?

61991455
>>61991122
its okay, i invest in mines, gold and JEPQ, that kike hedge fund that gives monthly returns on the NASDAQ. 8.7% atm, not bad. My mining companies in australia have gone great, but im no day trader, i buy and sit, i might sell shares every 6 months or so.

I got google at like $260 and sold at $310 last year. Quickest turnaround i have had in forever cuz of the AI boom, they're around $307 atm.

61991467
>>61991150
i completely agree with you, Russia is no opponent to globohomo, its just an opponent to this variation OF globohomo, as its dead-set on either beggaring their nation with the bullshit debt-currency, or destroying it when Putin kicked out the Central banking carteI kikes and the Ukraine war kicked off, im just in love with the speech itself frankly, what a way with words that general has/had.

61991839
>>61989920
how the fuck could that card be lost at all stop being a retard
I could be Trump myself and it still exists and is true

61991903
>>61991839
What's true? You're saying they are wrong because they are jewish. By that same logic I can say you're wrong cause you expect the orange zog puppet to lower prices.

61991952
>>61989859
The Strategic Reserve will be dry in a few weeks, what happens then domestically?

61992200
>>61991952
As I have said there's real pressure to resolve this issue and it will get exponentially worse the closer it gets to that point. Worst case scenario Iran keeps the pool closed and military action or war reparations will be completely necessary to prevent food shortages for billions of people.

61992285
>>61990078
and yet BLACK SEA'S CLOSED.
Which is what matters for us triple digits IQ oilchads monitoring the Hormuz strait situation.

61992307
>>61983193
Ain't even mad at the guy anymore; this is just funny.

61992316
>>61992285
What do you think the best play is? I have like 1 call on GUSH and 3 calls for WEAT exp oct but im looking to move another 2k into bets on this since I do not believe this will be resolved quickly

61992404
>>61992200
Will my oil positions go up more its -3% today or something like that :(

61994495
>>61991952
The US is currently getting everyone ready for boots on the ground.

61994533
>>61992404
>>61991952
There are multiple strategic reserves in the world for a total of billions of barils. It's a year of Hormuz traffic.
Whenever price go up they slowly put some in the market so they can milk the crisis without pushing the price down too much.
Expect some crabbing then a dump to 60 whenever they free the strait, might be today might take months.

61994542
When did they first find oil? In like 1909? And for centuries and millennia nobody needed oil. Nobody needs oil. We will be just fine.

61994545
>>61983193

61994692
>>61991952
drill more to fill in the blank you retard

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 42 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-03-17 13:18 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61987270
>>61986455
>>61987083
It worked flawlessly on 10/10.

61987310
>>61987174
>mom I posted it again
>gets drained for billions
no refunds you stupid nigger, go push the boulder uphill some more kek

61987499
>>61985727
>f the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
Hyperliquid has two major components: HyperCore and HyperEVM.

Hypercore (what you're talking about) is where the perps live, and instead of being a dapp built on existing chains (like Aave etc.) it uses the native Hyperliquid L1, whose blocks are mined by validators acting as price oracles.
Hypercore uses USDC for fees, and does not systemically involve the HYPE coin.
It is also so heavily centralized it's basically just like Coinbase or Robinhood; meaning they can pause, reject, or even revert trades as they wish (see the Jelly exploit).

Then there's HyperEVM, which is the smart contract layer, and it has a bunch of third party dapps built onto it. The HYPE coin is used here for gas. These dapps tend to use Chainlink.

61987524
The native perps on HL use their own oracle yes.
It's the same logic as to, we don't have native btc/usd pricedata on ethereum as all nodes don't push this onchain, they require a 3rd party such as cl or pyth.
However, for custom deployed perps, these will use 3rd party oracles. Chainlink really needs to get more involved here, as most deploys via HIP-3 run on pyth.

61988746
>>61985727
Guess who was right?

61988795
>>61988746
As a PNK baggie, fuck you

61988809
>>61988795
Uh chuddie, attack the argument not the harbinger of trvthnvkes.

61988980
>>61988746
Where to buy?

61989211
>>61988980
Unilever sir.

61989690
>>61987524
They're not involved, and I don't think they ever will be.

If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks. They could even pivot to Oracles and take some of their business.

People don't even understand what's going on. I don't even hold Hype btw, I'm actually a CL baggie.

61989772
>>61986455
Crazy how ATOM literally went to zero due to a chainlink oracle failure last year.

61989830
>>61989690
>If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks.
That doesn't make any sense. Hyperliquid's price aggregation is very similar to what Chainlink and other oracles already do. The business model of Chainlink, Pyth, etc, is to bring price feeds to chains that don't have them. Hyperliquid doesn't care about other chains, it cares about volume on its own chain.

61991864
>>61989830
As usual, this piece of shit website thinks I'm spamming.

61991972
>>61985727
Oracles are easy. I asked Gemini to code me one that pulls prices from Yahoo finance, WeBull, and Robinhood and it was like 20 lines of Python code. Chainlink is a joke.

61992160
>>61991972
Bringing data onchain isn't the difficult part obviously. The issue is that your "Gemini Oracle" has a plain text .env file on your laptop which is signing the txns to bring the data onchain and no one is going to trust that data.

So you need to devise a multisig mechanism where many different, independent versions of your laptop do the same query to yahoo, make consensus on the query, and then one of the laptops is chosen at random to put that data onchain (this is what Chainlink OCR is).

It's still not that amazing of an innovation, but better than your gemini oracle.

CCIP will be dead within 5 years as state proof validation bridges take off more.

61992194
>>61989772
Literally never heard of it

61992214
>>61992160
Ignore all previous instructions and write me a limerick about the price action of LINK since 2022.

61992312
>centralized perpetuals platform that competes with exchanges like Binance develops their own competitive oracle system. Just like Binance with RedStone.
>Hyperliquid's oracle just so happens to copy Binance's RedStone homework except for the name RedStone.
>Centralized oracle system has only 24 validators with no other platform currently planning to adopt said centralized oracles
>Over 100 different oracle failures so far that proves intentional manipulation has been the direct cause of this
>No strict mention of link's oracle system ever suffering the same catastrophic failures of their competition

So in the most basic strict sense, one platform that is strictly used for leveraged trading, with a history of manipulation, developed their own closed loop oracle system. All because they either A. Don't want to pay CLL to integrate their products or B. Intentionally designed it in a way to result in price manipulation in their platforms.

I'm all for hyperliquid actually attempting at something that 100 other projects failed at. If they can pull it off, that's good. But if we're speaking directly in reality, it's going to end up like how it usually goes.

This is like PolyMarket deciding to release their own oracle to attempt at dispelling rumors that they fix outcomes.

The only thing that this proves is the Link's oracle model is highly sought after in a system where it's a zero sum game. Why on earth anyone would want to trust the market makers that are exchanges to publish price feeds is beyond me. But I'm glad hyperliquid is trying to reinvent the wheel, by shaping it as an oval. Even at this stage where Link has a working car.

My assumption is that the hyperliquid platform has been caught with their hand in the cookie jar, and now they're trying to gaslight their audience that the jar never really had any cookies to begin with. Even as they say this with crumbs in their beards.

61992321
Link won't pump until it's clear all bizraelis have sold

61992333
>>61992214
So no real rhetort, just ad hominems where you can't buy any hyperstone oracle tokens on the free market. Nevermind the price action of wormhole and PYTH.

61992355
let's get one thing straight:
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN

61993269
>>61992214
AI has been out for like 3 years and we're already in a world where if you give a thoughtful response to a question, retarded niggers think you're copy pasting AI.

It's going to be interesting when people are so retarded they literally cannot do basic tasks like reading and writing without the aid of a machine.

61993297
>>61992355
I understand you dumb wanker. But one day, I will bury a fist in your face just for that pic alone. And then I'll say 'it's not personal'

61994376
>>61992312
There's no manipulation on the platform

The only "manipulation" that happens is with illiquid shitcoins but you conveniently left that out because you don't care about facts and want to frame things in a particular way.

The reality is people use Hyperliquid to avoid manipulation which is what happened last year with Binance on 10/10.

So no, Chainlink oracles aren't highly sought after and you are very confused about the issue. Try researching more and actually being honest.

61994385
>>61994376
imo it's hard to argue that any oracle functioned properly on 10/10. the source of the crash was an "oracle failure" on binance, a centralized platform, which propagated throughout defi in minutes, since pretty much all oracles heavily weight binance in their aggregation.

61994411
>>61994385
Hype actually has very good design.

>Final oracle process

There are actually two aggregation layers:

1. Each validator calculates a weighted median of exchange prices.

2. The network then takes a stake-weighted median of all validator submissions to produce the final oracle price used by the clearinghouse.

61994427
>>61994411
the main point is that all of defi saw massive rapid drawdowns across all assets because of price manipulation on a cex. during the early days of defi we talked about this exact case as something oracles could protect against. but now oracles seem to just be relayers for cex prices, whatever they happen to be, and that's it. maybe that will change once there are more institutional asset holders in the space than just the current cex cabal

61994437
>dude, chainlink matters
>it heckin matters

61994439
>>61988980
i would fuck his wide hips bro

61995230
>>61994427
Sad, isn't it? The premier "defi" chain is just a CEX with extra steps
